ety: act. part. of افلس 'to become insolvent; to be reduced to poverty,' iv of فلس not in use; see fals, and fulūs
def: adj. & s.m. Insolvent, bankrupt; penniless; indigent, needy, in want, poor, wretched, beggarly; — an insolvent; a penniless person, a pauper; — (as used by Europeans) a bachelor
more: adj. فلس Penniless; indigent ( garīb , 1). muflis ; Illit. mukhlis , n. m. 1. A pauper. 1. Muflis hameshah khuār . Prov. To be poor is to be in disgrace. 2. Muflis kī jorū sadā naṅgī . Prov. The poor man's wife is always naked. 3. Muflis se savāl karnā harām hai . Prov. Saying. It is a sin to beg of a poor man. 4. Kab lagātā hai koī is dil-i-be-hāl kā mol? Sab ghaṭā dete haiṅ muflis ke garaz māl kā mol . Sūrūr. This wretched heart at its fair value none will own, The poor man's wares by all disparaged and beat down. 2. An insolvent. 3. ( Europeans ) A bachelor. muflis k., kar denā, yā banā denā , v. a. To impoverish; make poor.
(part. act. IV. of فلس Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Poor, wretched, beggarly; bankrupt.
subs: muflis banā-denā or muflis karnā or kar-denā
v.t. To make poor, reduce to poverty, to impoverish.

def: (for A. مفلسة muflisat, fem. of muflis), adj. f. = muflis, q.v. adj. & s.m. Insolvent, bankrupt; penniless; indigent, needy, in want, poor, wretched, beggarly; — an insolvent; a penniless person, a pauper; — (as used by Europeans) a bachelor
subs: muflis banā-denā or muflis karnā or kar-denā
v.t. To make poor, reduce to poverty, to impoverish.

ety: fr. muflis
def: s.f. Insolvency, bankruptcy; indigence, poverty, pauperism
more: n. f. Pauperism; poverty; indigence. Muflisī aisī ham pe chhāī hai Sir pe ṭopī bhī to parāī hai . Black poverty her pall hath o'er me spread, Borrowed the very cap upon my head. muflisī chhānā , v. n. To be steeped in poverty.
subs: (-ko), Poverty to overshadow (one), to be sunk in poverty.

ety: pass. part. of افرد 'to make (a person, or thing
def: adj. & s.m. Single, sole; solitary, alone; — singular; unique, simple; uncompounded; — (in Gram., &c.) a single, or simple, word or vocable.
more: (part. pass. IV. of فرد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Solitary, alone, single, simple, singular. Plur. مفردات mufradāt, Simples, uncompounded (medicines).

def: s.m. (pl. of mufrad, or of its fem. mufradat), Simples (in medicine), uncompounded medicines.

ety: act. part. of افرط 'to exceed due bounds; to be or become excessive,' &c., iv of فرط 'to precede,' &c.
def: adj. Exceeding the due bounds, or just limits; — excessive, vast; — transcendent, transcendental.
more: (part. act. IV. of فرط) adj. Excessive, vast.

ety: act. part. of افسد 'to make or render bad, evil, or corrupt,' &c., iv of فسد 'to be or become bad,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Mischievous, factious, seditious, turbulent; pernicious, destructive, corruptive; noxious; vitiating; — a factious or seditious person (syn. fasād karne-wālā), a mischief-maker, an incendiary; depraver, corrupter; malefactor, &c.
more: adj. فسد Factious; mischievous (دنگئي 2, 3). mufsid , n. m. An incediary; a mischief maker; ringleader ( āg-lagāu , 2). majmaë-i-mufsidān , A riotous assembly; mob. mufsidā'nah , adv. 1. Riotously. 2. Feloniously.
(part. act. IV. of فسد) A seditious person, author of evil, mischiefmaker, mischievous, noxious, incendiary. مفسدپن mufsid-pan, s. m. Litigiousness, seditiousness.
subs: s.m. A company of factious or seditious persons, &c.; a riotous assembly; a mob.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُفْسِد (mufsid).

ety: mufsid, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adv. Seditiously; riotously; feloniously.

adj. = mufsid, q.v.; — s.f. = mufsid-pan, q.v. adj. & s.m. Mischievous, factious, seditious, turbulent; pernicious, destructive, corruptive; noxious; vitiating; — a factious or seditious person (syn. fasād karne-wālā), a mischief-maker, an incendiary; depraver, corrupter; malefactor, &c.
subs: s.m. A company of factious or seditious persons, &c.; a riotous assembly; a mob.

ety: mufsid, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Factiousness, seditiousness; mischievousness; perniciousness; — litigiousness.

adj. & adv. Gratuitous; acquired without cost or labour; given away without return or benefit; gratuitously, gratis, without payment, for nothing; — in vain, unprofitably, uselessly; — s.m. What is useless or unprofitable
more: adv. 1. Gratuitously; for nothing (سينت). 1. Muft rā che guft? P. Prov. You cannot murmur at a gift. (You must not look a gift horse in the mouth). 2. Muft kā māl kis ko burā lagtā hai? Prov. A gift is acceptable to all. 2. In vain; unprofitably; uselessly. Jis ne sir nīṅd chaṛhāī, us ne muphtan umar gaṅvāī . Rus. Who hath to sleep given way in vain hath passed his life away, muft-khorā , n. m. A loafer. Do kauṛiyāṅ muft-khore ko bhī! Mendicants. Two kauris for the beggar too! muft meṅ kām karānā , v. a. 1. To get work done for nothing. 2. To press; impress.

adv. Gratuitously, gratis, for nothing, acquired without cost or labour, given away without return or benefit. (substantively) What is useless or unprofitable. مفت بر muft-bar, Taking away for nothing or without making a return. مفت مين muft meṅ, Gratis, for nothing.
subs: adj. Taking away for nothing, or without making a return
or muft-ḵẖorā, s.m. One who eats for nothing, a parasite, sponger, hanger on; a loafer
'What is to be said in respect of a thing obtained for nothing?'; one cannot murmur at a gift; one must not look a gift-horse in the mouth
s.m. A thing obtained for nothing; a gift
To get work done for nothing; — to press, impress.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مفت (muft).

ety: act. part. of افتقر, viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. Becoming poor or needy; reduced to poverty.
more: (part. act. VIII. of فقر) adj. Empoverished, reduced to poverty.

ety: pass. part. of افتر 'to make (one
def: adj. Rendered languid; languid; enfeebled; weak.
more: (part. pass. IV. of فتر) adj. Enfeebled, languid, weak.

ety: for A. مفترٍ muftariṉ, act. part. of افتري 'to forge or fabricate a lie or falsehood,' viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. & s.m. Forging or fabricating falsehood; knavish, tricky; — a liar; slanderer, false accuser; a knave, rogue.
more: (part. act. VIII. of فري Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A slanderer, calumniator, false accuser, liar.

ety: muft, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Gratuitous.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُفْتی (muftī), from Arabic مُفْتٍ (muftin).

ety: for A. مفتٍ muftiṉ, act. part. of افتي 'to notify the decision of the law,' iv of فتو; see fatwā
def: s.m. A jurisconsult who notifies the decisions of the (Muhammadan) law, in, or respecting, cases submitted to him; an expounder of Muhammadan law, a Mufti
more: n. m. فتو A Mah. jurist or law officer. Kyā muftī kī le āūṅ? (Mazāq of Players). Shall I bring her for nothing? (A satire on a close-fisted Muftī ).
(part. act. IV. of فتو) s. m. A superiour officer among Muhammadans whose decrees are regarded as law, Mufti. مفتي گري muftī-garī, s. f. Office or duty of muftī.
subs: s.f. Office or duty of a Mufti.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُفْتی (muftī), from Arabic مُفْتٍ (muftin).

ety: old P. magu; Pehl. magu; Zend moghu; cf. S. मघवन्
def: s.m. One of the Magi; a worshipper of fire; — a tavern-keeper (plur. mug̠ān)
more: s. m. A tavernkeeper, or one of the Magi, a worshipper of fire. مغبچه mug̠ẖ-bacha, The son of a tavernkeeper, or worshipper of fire. (Plur. مغان).
subs: s.m. The son of a (or a young) fire-worshipper; the son of a tavern-keeper.

ety: dialec., or corr.
def: s.f. = mūg = mūṅg, q.v. s.m. One of the Magi; a worshipper of fire; — a tavern-keeper (plur. mug̠ān)
subs: s.m. The son of a (or a young) fire-worshipper; the son of a tavern-keeper.

ety: pass. part. of غيّر 'to conceal,' &c., ii of عاب (for غيب
def: part. adj. Hidden, concealed; invisible.
more: (part. pass. II. of غيب Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Concealed, hidden.

ety: pass. part. of غيّر 'to alter, change,' ii of غار (for غير
def: part. adj. Altered, changed.
more: (part. pass. II. of غير Heb. ) adj. Changed, altered.

ety: for A. مغالطة mug̠ālat̤at, inf. n. of غالط 'to vie with in endeavouring to cause each the other to make a mistake,' iii of غلط 'to make a mistake'
def: s.m. Leading into error; — deception; delusion; — ambiguity; — error
more: n. m. غلط See dhokā , (1, 2). 1. Leading into error. 2. Deception; delusion. 3. Error ( bhūl , 4). mugāltah-dihī k . To mistake; misreport. mugāltah denā , v. a. 1. To misinform; mislead. 2. To practise deception; to balk; delude.
(inf. III. of غلط) s. m. Leading into errour, delusion, ambiguity.
subs: s.f. Misleading; practising deception (on); misinforming; misreporting; — mistaking
(-ko), To lead into error, to mislead, to practise deception (on), to deceive, delude; to misinform.

ety: pass. part. of غلّظ 'to render gross, or hard, or rigorous,' &c., ii of غلظ 'to be or become thick, or gross, or hard,' &c.
def: adj. Hard, rigorous, severe, heavy (as a mulct for bloodshed, &c.); — made hard to be broken, solemn, binding (an oath); — very foul, impure, offensive (language).

ety: for A. مغلظة mug̠allaz̤at, fem. of mug̠allaz̤
def: part. adj. = mug̠allaz̤, q.v. s.m. One of the Magi; a worshipper of fire; — a tavern-keeper (plur. mug̠ān)
more: (part. pass. fem. II. of غلظ) adj. Rendered hard to be broken (an oath), or heavy (a fine for blood).
subs: s.m. The son of a (or a young) fire-worshipper; the son of a tavern-keeper.

r: mug̠allaz̤āt, mug̠allaz̤at, mug̠allaz̤, mug̠allaz̤āt baknā, mug̠allaz̤āt sunānā, ko
def: s.f. pl. (of mug̠allaz̤at, fem. of mug̠allaz̤), Foul or offensive language, abuse
more: n. f. pl. of غليظ Foul or abusive language. mugallazāt baknā, yā sunānā , v. a. To use foul language.
subs: or mug̠allaz̤āt sunānā (-ko), To use foul language, or abuse.

def: s.m. A tavern, a house of promiscuous entertainment; — (pl. of mug̠, q.v.), s.m. Magi, fire-worshippers; — a fraternity of Christian monks.

ety: caus. of mugnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To cause to become musty or spoiled (as grain, &c.).

ety: for A. مغنٍّ mug̠anniṉ, act. part. of غنّي 'to sing, chant,' &c., ii of غني 'to be content,' &c.
def: s.m. A singer; a musician; — part. adj. Making free from want; rendering in a state of competence, or rich, or independent.
more: (part. act. II. of غني) s. m. A singer, a musician. ¶ adj. Causing independence, making independent.

ety: pass. part. of غرّق to immerse' (in
def: part. Sunk (in), dipped or immersed (in); covered (with); gilded; ornamented with gold or silver; plated.

ety: for A. مغايرة, inf. n. of غاير 'to barter,' &c., iii of غار (for غير
def: s.f. Bartering, exchanging; barter, exchange; — difference, disagreement; contrariety, repugnance.
more: (inf. III. of غير) s. f. 1. Barter, exchange. 2. Disagreement, contrariety, repugnance.

ety: act. part. of غاير 'to differ, each from the other,' iii of غار (for غير
def: adj. Different; contrary; repugnant; adverse.
more: (part. act. III. of غير Heb. ) adj. Contrary, adverse, repugnant.

def: s.m. Soreness under the arms.

adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = mugdha, and mukdhā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Stupefied; stupid, ignorant; infatuated, enamoured (of), in love (with); besotted; foolish, silly; simple, artless; mistaken, erring, led astray; — charming, lovely, beautiful; — a stupid or ignorant person, a fool, simpleton, an idiot, &c.
subs: s.m. 'Instructing the ignorant'; name of a celebrated Sanskrit grammar by Vopadev.

ety: S. मुद्गरः
def: s.m. A mallet (i.q. mogrā, q.v.); — an athlete's club (for exercising the muscles), a dumb-bell; (pl.) dumb-bells
more: n. m. S. मुद्दर, Pr. muggaro hammer. Dumbbells. mugdar hilānā, ghumānā, yā phirānā , W.; mugdar bhāṅjnā, bhānnā , E. v. n. To exercise with dumbbells.
s. A mallet; a club used to exercise with in the manner of dumb bells.
subs: or ghumānā, or hilānā, (dialec.) mugdar bhāṅjnā or bhānnā, To turn or swing dumb-bells; to exercise with dumb-bells.

def: s.f. A silly or foolish woman; — a young and beautiful woman; — a virgin.
more: s. f. A virgin, a young woman.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Stupefied; stupid, ignorant; infatuated, enamoured (of), in love (with); besotted; foolish, silly; simple, artless; mistaken, erring, led astray; — charming, lovely, beautiful; — a stupid or ignorant person, a fool, simpleton, an idiot, &c.
subs: s.m. 'Instructing the ignorant'; name of a celebrated Sanskrit grammar by Vopadev.

ety: mugdhā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Virginity.
s.f. = S مگدهتو मुग्धत्व mugdhatva, s.m. Stupidity, ignorance; foolishness, folly, silliness; — simplicity, artlessness; — loveliness, charmingness, gracefulness.
s.m. = S مگدهتا मुग्धता mugdhatā, s.f. Stupidity, ignorance; foolishness, folly, silliness; — simplicity, artlessness; — loveliness, charmingness, gracefulness.

ety: fr. mugnā, q.v.
def: adj. (dialec.; f. -ī), Musty (as grain, &c.).
adj. Secret; hidden. muggham rahnā, ghar ke ghar rahnā , v. n. ( Gamblers ) To be quits. muggham meṅ kahnā , v. n. To speak in innuendoes.
s. m. Soreness under the arms.
adj. Of the Moghuls.

r: mug̠īlān, mug̠ailān, ummu g̠īlān, jinn, g̠īlān, g̠ūl
ety: corr. fr. A. امُّ غيلان ummu g̠īlān, 'the mother of goblins, or jinn'; g̠īlān being the pl. of g̠ūl, q.v.
def: s.m. The Egyptian or Arabian thorn; a species of Mimosa or Acacia (syn. babūl; kīkar, which, as is the mug̠īlān, are believed to be haunted by evil spirits).
more: s. Name of a tree, called in Hindī ببول babūl and کيکڙ kīkaṛ, q. v. and in Arab. ام غيلان ummu g̠ẖailān, a species of Mimosa, or the acacia tree.

ety: fr. mug̠al
def: adj. Having gold and silver lace.

ety: pass. part. of اغلق 'to make fast, to lock, to close,' &c., iv of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Abstruse, recondite; abstract; obscure; knotty, intricate, very hard or difficult.
more: adj. غلق 1. Obscure. 2. Abstruse. 3. Very hard or difficult.
(part. pass. IV. of غلق) adj. Abstruse, knotty, obscure, intricate.

ety: mug̠al, q.v.+S. aff. आनी
def: s.f. A woman of the Mogul class; — the wife of a Mogul; — an attendant in the harem or women's apartment; — a needle-woman, sempstress.
more: n. f. 1. A Mugal woman. 2. An attendant in the harem or women's apartments. 3. A needlewoman; sempstress. mugla'ī , adj. Having gold and silver lace. muglī hāṛ , n. m. A large bone. muglī bīmārī ; H. kameṛe , n. f. Convulsions (Children).
s. f. A female mug̠ẖul; an attendant in the harem or womenʼs apartment, a needle-woman.

ety: act. part. of اغلم 'to excite lust,' &c., iv of غلم 'to be or become excited by lust'
def: adj. & s.m. Libidinous; — a pederast.
more: (part. act. IV. of غلم Heb. and Aram. ) 1. adj. Libidinous. 2. (subst.) A pæderast.

ety: mug˚ = Prk. मुग्ग = S. मुद्ग; see mūṅg
def: v.n. (dialec.) To smell like mūṅg (used of grain kept long in a damp granary); to become musty (as grain, &c.).
more: v. n. (dakh.) To grow musty ( Harris ).

def: s.m. = mogrā, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A mallet, pounder, rammer; a washerman's mallet (for beating clothes); a ramrod; the dog-head nail of the cock of a flint-lock; — the double jasmine, Jasminum zambac, var. γ (called also Tuscan jasmine, and 'the double Moogrie').

ety: pass. part. of اغرق 'to immerse' (in

more: (part. pass. IV. of غرق) adj. Gilded, covered with (or immersed in) gold or silver. ¶ mug̠ẖarraḳ (part. pass. II.) adj. Immersed (in water), washed (with gold, silver, &c.), gilt, plated, dipped.

ety: act. part. of اغتنم 'to reckon (a thing
def: part. adj. Considering, or holding, as spoil or prey, or as a prize, or blessing; — enriched, endowed with abundance; possessing, enjoying, taking delight (in)
more: (part. act. VIII. of غنم) adj. Holding and accounting as a prize or blessing, enriched, taking delight in, enjoying. ¶ mug̠ẖtanam (part. pass.) Accounted a prize or blessing.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Accounted an invaluable prize; regarded as a blessing.

def: s.m. A Mogul; — the third of the four classes into which Muḥammadans are divided
more: n. m. muglānī , n. f. 1. A Mogal. 1. Mār līyā hai Mugle ko! (Said of Nādar Shāh). 2. Mugal bā-dagal . Prov. Saying. Mogal cheat! 2. The third sect of Mahomedans. mugal paṭhān , n. m. A game like draughts, played with sixteen shells or pebbles on a diagram sketched on the ground, or on a board, or paper. mugal paṭhān laṛnā , Mah. Wom. The sound made in boiling or simmering ( khadar badar h .). kāblī mugal , n. m. An idle disputant.

s. m. A Moghul. مغل پٿهان mug̠ẖul-paṭhān, A game played like that of draughts on a diagram sketched on the ground, or on a board or paper, using sixteen kauṛīs or bits of gravel or pebbles, &c. on each side for men. ( Herklots ).
subs: s.m. 'The Mogul and the Paṭhān'; a kind of game played (like draughts) on a diagram sketched on the ground, or on a board or paper, with sixteen kauṛīs, or pebbles, &c. used on each side for men
s.m. A Mogul of Kābul; — an idle disputant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُغُل (muġul), ultimately from Middle Mongol ᠮᠣᠩᠬᠣᠯ (mongqol).

ety: mug̠ul, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Of or relating to the Moguls; Mogul, Tartar; — (met.) fearless; cruel; severe, terrible, &c.
subs: s.m. A bone like a Mogul's; — a large bone.

(kisī-ke), To fall into the mouth (of); — to be eaten; — to behave with boldness or presumption (before)
adj. f. (m. -paṛā), On the tongues (of people; — syn. zabān-zad)
v.n. To open the mouth wide, to gape (with wonder, &c.)
adj. & s.m. Outspoken, fearless, unconcerned; foul-mouthed, abusive; — an outspoken, or fearless, &c. person; — a vain babbler
v.n. The face to be turned or distorted, the face to be paralyzed; — the face to be turned away (from, -se), to be satiated or cloyed; to be disgusted (with); to be displeased (with)
To distend the cheeks; to make a wry face, &c. (i.q. muṅh bigāṛnā, q.v.)
'To burst open the lips and speak'; to break silence; to make bold to say

To gape (i.q. muṅh bānā, and muṅh pasārnā), to yawn; — to desire much; — to presume; — to demand a high price (for); to raise one's price

muṅh thutānā or thuthānā = muṅh banānā

To distort the face; to make wry faces (see muṅh bigāṛnā)
'To join face (to face),' to draw close together and whisper; to talk scandal in an under-tone
(-kā), To lick the face (of); to caress; to make much (of), to curry favour (with), to toady
(-kā), To make mouths or faces (at), to mock; — to make a bad imitation (of), to caricature

To bite, to snap (as a horse); to open the mouth (to devour); — to work the mouth or jaws, to eat
The mouth to be moving or working; to chew the cud; — to be foul-mouthed
adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, bashful, timid; — keeping at home, not visiting friends
(-se), To hide or veil the face (from bashfulness, &c.); to keep parda; — to be bashful; — to be in hiding, to hide, skulk; — to avoid, shun, to evade
s.m. & f. One who hides his (or her) face; — a bashful person; — a skulker, &c.; — blind-man's buff
(-kā), To touch the face (of); — to give (one) a formal invitation (to)
adv. Face to face, before, in the presence (of)
(-ko), To show one's face (to), to appear before (one) with confidence and satisfaction (e.g. kyā muṅh dikhāʼūṅgā? 'how can I show my face?')
s.f. A present given (by the female relations of the bridegroom, and the friends of the family) on the first visit to a bride
(-ke, &c.), To continue staring at (one) with surprise, to be lost in astonishment
To say pleasant things (to), to flatter

(apnā, or kisī-kā, &c.), To see one's face (in a glass); — to look at the face (of); to look about idly; — to stare at in astonishment or helplessness; — to look to (one, for aid, &c.), to depend (on); — to have a regard (for one)
or prīt, or ćāh, or maḥabbat, Show of regard or friendship before a person; apparent friendship or affection
muṅh-dekhī bāt kahnā = muṅh dekh-kar bāt kahnā
(-meṅ), To put the mouth or lips (into, or to); — to make familiarized, to favour, to countenance
(-meṅ), To put the mouth (into, or to); to thrust the muzzle (into); to eat; — to bite (a horse); to peck or seize with the beak (a cock, in fighting); — to beg, request
To keep a good countenance; to keep on good terms (with)
adj. Verbal (message, or evidence, &c.), oral, vivâvoce
'The face to become yellow,' to turn pale (from fear)
Hard-mouthed (a horse); restive; — headstrong, stubborn, obstinate; — outspoken, &c. (i.q. muṅh-phaṭ, q.v.)
s.f. Hardness of mouth; headstrongness, &c.; — foul-mouthedness, abusiveness
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, to sulk; — to cause the face (of another, -kā, &c.) to swell (by a slap, or blow; — i.q. muṅh phulānā, and muṅh bigāṛnā)
To contract the face, to make a wry face
To bridle the mouth or tongue; to hold one's tongue
The mouth to become dry or parched; — the face to become thin or emaciated

Flowers (of rhetoric) to fall from the lips (of, -ke); — (ironic.) to rate in round terms, to reproach, to abuse
Milk to drop from the mouth (of); to be yet a child, to have no sense
(-kā), To sew up the mouth (of); — to close or seal the lips (of, i.q. muṅh-par mǒhr karnā); to silence (a person); — to give hush-money (to, i.q. muṅh bharnā)
(-kā), To change colour, to turn pale (esp. from fear)
To blacken one's own face; to incur disgrace; — to blacken the face (of another, -kā); to disgrace, bring disgrace on; to punish; — to turn out with disgrace; to break off all connection (with)
The face to be or become black or blackened; to incur disgrace, be disgraced
muṅh-kā laććhan jhaṛnā = muṅh-kī loʼī utarnā
'With the mouth's (morsel) in the mouth and the hand's (spoonful) in the hand'; staggered, aghast
s.m. A morsel; — anything easily attained

(-ke), To get a taste for blood; vice to grow (on one)
The mouth to become open; — to become foul-mouthed or abusive
(-kā), To cause (one's) mouth to become opened; to make (or to provoke one to) speak
To open the mouth; — to abuse; — to uncover the face; to present to view; to reveal
(or bhal) girnā, To fall on the face, or face foremost
muṅh-ke kawwe uṛ-jānā = muṅh-par fāḵẖta uṛ-jānā
To eat one's own words; to be convicted out of one's own mouth; — to be browbeaten; — to receive a blow on the face; — to suffer disgrace; to be put to the blush
(-ke), To plug the mouth (of), to gag; to silence; — to tie the tongue (of a person) with a charm or spell; — to exorcise

To redden the mouth or lips; — to make the face (of another, -kā) red (by slapping)

'To muffle up the face and lie down'; to lie down in the sulks; to take to one's bed in grief
To hang down the face or head; — to make a long face, to be down in the mouth
To apply the mouth or lips (to, -meṅ)

(-ke, or mere, &c.), To have the mouth burned or affected (by a pungent substance, &c.); — to suit the mouth or palate (of), to be agreeable to the taste (of); — to become used, or accustomed (to), or familiarized (with); — to become intimate (with), to become a favourite (of); — to dispute, or bandy words (with)
'To attack (one) with the mouth,' to bite; to sting; to say biting or stinging things; — to attack, to fall upon; — to stop the mouth (of a person, -kā); to silence; — to feed
or muṅh-māgā, adj. (f. -ī), Demanded, requested, or asked for by word of mouth
s.m. Price demanded or asked for; — muṅh-māṅgī murād, s.f. Any thing or object obtained in accordance with expressed wish; — heart-felt wish
s.f. The death asked or wished for
To make a wry face; — to turn up the nose; — to frown, to be displeased, or offended

(-se), To turn away the face (from); to disregard; to be averse (to); to avoid, shun; to abstain or desist (from); — to turn (against), to rebel, revolt; — to turn a deaf ear (to); to give the cold shoulder (to)
(-kā), To sweeten the mouth (of); to give (one) a treat; — to give a sop or bribe (to)
The mouth to water; to feel eager desire (for); — to be tempted, or seduced

To read to oneself, to mumble
To take a straw in one's mouth (as a sign of humility, or submission); to humble or abase oneself, to lick the dust; — to submit (to), resign oneself (to)
s.m.f.(?) The mouth-piece of a ḥuqqa
To look blank or foolish; — to simper
v.n. Not to have the face (to)

'The face and hands to break'; the limbs to be broken; to meet with a bad accident
To wash the face and hands
To return (from any enterprise) with a face (expressive of disappointment) peculiarly one's own; to return disappointed; to fail of success
To put the hand to the mouth (in admiration, or astonishment, or shame) and remain motionless; to be astonished, or ashamed, &c.
or ḵẖāk tere muṅh-meṅ, Dirt in your mouth! may your mouth be fouled!
(May his, or your,) face (become) black! may disgrace overtake him!

def: s. m. See منـﮩ muṅh.
(old H.), pron. obl. = mo-hi (q.v.s.v. mo), and mujh, q.v. (Braj), 1˚ pron. obl., see s.v. mo; — 2˚ = mohit, q.v. pron. obl. (Braj, or dialec.) Me (i.q. mujh); — of me, my (i.q. merā)
subs: On or upon me (i.q. mujh-par); — by me, in my possession (i.q. mere-pās)
moro, morau, morauṅ, Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā)

Like me (i.q. mujh-sā, or mere samān)
Of me, my, mine (i.q. merā; see kar)
mo-kau, mo-koṅ, mo-ko, mo-kāṅ, mo-kā, To me; me (i.q. mujh-ko; mujhe)
To me; me (i.q. mujhe); — mo-hi, or mo-hī, Me indeed; to me truly, &c. (i.q. mujh-hī).

ety: for A. محاباة muḥābāt, inf. n. of حابی 'to aid or assist; to treat with partiality; to deal gently or leniently with,' iii of حبو 'to draw near (to
def: s.m. Partiality (for); lenient or gentle treatment, kind behaviour; respect, regard, friendship, affection; — caution, care
more: (inf. III. of حبو) s. m. Respect, regard, friendship, affection. بي محابا be muḥābā, adj. Without respect (of persons or circumstances).
subs: Without partiality: without respect of persons or circumstances); without gentleness or lenience; unmercifully.

ety: pass. part. of حدّب 'to make gibbous or convex,' ii of حدب 'to be or become gibbous or convex'
def: adj. Gibbous; convex.

ety: act. part. of حدّث 'to tell, relate; to relate a story or tradition,' ii of حدث 'to be new or recent,' &c.; see ḥadīs̤
def: s.m. One who produces something new; an inventor; an innovator; — a narrator, relater; — a collector, or compiler, or reporter of Muhammadan traditions; a believer in tradition; one well versed in the ḥadīs̤, q.v.
more: Mah. n. m. One well-versed in the hadīs , q. v.
(part. act. II. of حدث) s. A relater, narrator.

ety: corr. of A. محفة miḥaffat, n. of place fr. حفّ 'to surround (with
def: s.m. A kind of litter (in which women travel), a palkī.
more: n. m. A palankin, esp. for women.
(corrupt. of محفه miḥaffa, from حفّ Heb. or ) s. m. A pālkī, litter in which women travel.

ety: for A. محافظة, inf. n. of حافظ; see muḥāfiz
def: s.f. Defending, protecting, guarding; keeping in custody; — preservation, protection, guardianship; custody, care.
more: n. f. حفظ preserved. 1. Guardianship; protection. Jān yā māl kī muhāfizat . 2. Custody; care.
(inf. III. of حفظ) s. f. Preservation, protection, guardianship, custody, care.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian محافظت (muhāfazat), from Arabic مُحَافَظَة (muḥāfaẓa).

ety: act. part. of حافظ 'to defend, to preserve,' &c., iii of حفظ 'to keep, preserve, guard,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Defending; keeping, guarding, &c.; — defender, protector, keeper, guardian, custodian; curator; watchman, guard, sentry; commander, governor, warden
more: n. m. حفظ preserving, guarding. 1. A watchman; guard; warden. 2. A keeper; guardian; curator. 3. A protector. muhāfiz-i-tan . Body guard. muhāfiz-i-haqīqī . The true protector; God. muhāfiz-khānāh , Record room. muhāfiz-daftar , A record keeper. muhafiz-i-mahbas , G. G. A jailor ( dārogah-i-jel khāna ).
part. pass. (III. of حفظ) Protected, defended, fortified.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Defended, protected; fortified
s.m. The true protector, the Deity
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُحَافِظ (muḥāfiẓ).

ety: pass. part. of هيّأ 'to set or dispose in order, &c.' ii of هاء (for هيأ
def: part. adj. Disposed in order, arranged; got together, got ready, prepared, ready
subs: v.t. To keep prepared or ready
v.t. To get together; to arrange; to make ready, to prepare.

def: adj. Got ready; prepared. muhaiyā k. , v. a. To make ready; procure.
more: (part. pass. II. of هيأة) adj. Prepared, ready, arranged.

ety: for A. مہاجرة, inf. n. of هاجر 'to abandon (friends, &c.
def: s.f. Abandoning (one's country, or friends); fleeing; desertion; flight; emigration; — separation; distance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُهاجَرَت (muhājarat), from Arabic مُهَاجَرَة (muhājara).

def: n. f. مـﮩـار separated. 1. Separation. 2. Emigration.
more: (inf. III. of هجر) s. f. Separation, distance, desertion, flight, abandoning (a country).

ety: act. part. of هاجر; see next
def: part. adj. & s.m. Abandoning, quitting, &c.; — one who quits (his country, especially on account of public calamity or persecution); a fugitive; an exile (pl. muhājirīn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُهَاجِر (muhājir), from Arabic مُهَاجِر (muhājir, “migrant”), from هَاجَرَ (hājara).

ety: for A. محققة muḥaqqaqat, fem. of muḥaqqaq
def: adj. = muḥaqqaq, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of حقّر 'to render contemptible or despised,' &c., ii of حقر 'to be contemptible,' &c.
def: part. adj. Despised, contemptible; — poor; low.
more: (part. pass. II. of حقر) adj. Despised.

ety: act. part. of حقّق, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Proving to be true, verifying, confirming; investigating the truth; inquisitive; — one who knows or regards as true; a verifier, an affirmer, a confirmer; — an asserter or teacher of truth, a philosopher; a writer (on some special subject), an authority (on); a critical judge (in matters of literature, &c.); — muḥaqqaq (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Ascertained, confirmed, verified, authenticated, certified, known to be true; decided, positive; — sound, compact, orderly (speech, or language); firmly or compactly woven (cloth).
more: adj. حق was right. 1. See تحقيق 2. Certified; verified; authenticated; confirmed. 3. Decided; positive. muhaq'qiq , n. m. A philosopher.

(part. pass. II. of حقّ) adj. Confirmed, certified, authenticated, verified, known for truth. ¶ muḥaḳḳiḳ (part. act.) adj. Verifying, proving to be true, investigating the truth, inquisitive; verifier.

ety: from muḥaqqiq
def: adv. Truly, accurately.
(obl. pl. of muḥaqqiq), s.m. pl. Philosophers; authorities, &c. (see muḥaqqiq).

ety: pass. part. of احال 'to be absurd, or impossible,' &c., iv of حول 'to become altered,' &c.
def: adj. Absurd; impossible; that cannot be
more: adj. Impossible. muhāl mutlaq . Absolutely impossible. shart-i-muhāl , An impossible condition.
subs: adj. Quite absurd; absolutely impossible; very difficult.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحَال (muhāl), borrowed from Arabic مُحَال (muḥāl), from أَحَالَ (ʔaḥāla). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi محال (mḥal /⁠muhāl⁠/).

def: s.m. pl. (of muḥāl), Absurdities; impossibilities.

ety: act. part. of حلّل, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Causing to dissolve, dissolving; (in Med.) discutient; — a solvent; one who solves, or resolves; — muḥallal (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Made lawful; lawful.
s.m. pl. (of muḥallil), Solvents; medicines which, by their subtilty, dissolve and dissipate any morbid and stagnant fluid.

ety: pass. part. of حمّد 'to praise much or repeatedly,' ii of حمد 'to praise'
def: part. Greatly (or highly) praised; — s.m. Name of the Arabian prophet; a proper name.
more: (part. pass. II. of حمد Heb. and Chald. ) 1. adj. Praised. 2. n. prop. Name of the Arabian prophet.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُحَمَّد (muhammad), from Arabic مُحَمَّد (muḥammad).

def: adj. Of Muḥammad; Muḥammadan.
wety: From مُحَمَّد (muḥammad, “Muhammad”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: S. मुख+अयन+कः, or मुख+स्थानकं; cf. gharānā
def: s.m. Mouth (of a river, &c.); an estuary; confluence (of two rivers); a strait; an inlet, or outlet.
n. m. S. मुख 1. See دﮩـانه 2. The mouth (of a river, etc.).
more: s. m. The mouth (of a river, &c.), inlet, outlet.

def: n. m. A geometrician.
more: (part. act. from هندسه handasa ) s. m. A geometrician.

ety: fr. handasa, corr. fr. P. andāza
def: s.m. Geometrician; a surveyor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مهندس (muhandis), from Arabic مُهَنْدِس (muhandis), from هَنْدَسَة (handasa, “engineering”), itself borrowed from Middle Persian.

def: s. f. The art of the geometrician, geometry.

ety: fr. muhandis
def: s.f. The art of the geometrician; — geometry.
wety: From Classical Persian مهندسی (muhandisī), from مهندس (muhandis), from Arabic مُهَنْدِس (muhandis), from هَنْدَسَة (handasa, “engineering”), from Middle Persian.

ety: prob. a dialec. var. of mahaknā, q.v.
def: v.t. To smell.
s.m. = P محاربت muḥārabat, s.f. (for A. محاربة muḥārabat, inf. n. of حارب; see muḥārib), Waging war, fighting; war, battle, combat, fight
more: n. f. 1. A combat; duel. 2. Fight; battle.
s. m. (inf. III. of حرب Heb. ) War, battle, combat, fight.

def: s.f. = P محاربه muḥāraba, s.m. (for A. محاربة muḥārabat, inf. n. of حارب; see muḥārib), Waging war, fighting; war, battle, combat, fight
more: s. f. (inf. III. of حرب Heb. ) War, battle, combat, fight.

ety: act. part. of حارب 'to wage war with, to contend with,' iii of حرب 'to despoil of, to plunder'
def: s.m. A warrior, combatant, fighter.
more: (part. act. III. of حرب) s. m. A warriour, a combatant, fighter.

ety: pass. part. of هرّأ 'to boil or cook thoroughly; to dissolve,' ii of هرأ 'to be well cooked,' &c.
def: part. adj. Well-dressed, well-boiled; dissolved; — pounded, comminuted.

ety: pass. part. of حرّف, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Inverted; transposed; — altered in form (a word); perverted (as language, &c.); tampered with.
more: (part. pass. II. of حرف Heb. ) adj. Inverted, transposed.

ety: pass. part. of حرّم 'to forbid, prohibit,' &c., ii of حرم 'to be forbidden,' &c.
def: part. adj. Forbidden, unlawful; excluded; sacred; venerable, dignified; — s.m. 'The sacred month,' the month of Muḥarram, the first month of the Muḥammadan year (held sacred on account of the death of Ḥusain, son of 'Alī, who was killed by Yazīd, near Kūfa); — mourning and lamentation
more: n. m. حرم prohibiting. 1. The first month of the Mah. year, held sacred on account of the death of Husain. 2. Mourning and lamentation. Yahāṅ to bārah mahīne muharram rahtā hai. muharram kā sipāhī . A holiday soldier. muharram kī paidāish . The Knight of the rueful countenance.

(part. pass. II. of حرم Heb. ) 1. adj. Sacred, venerable, forbidden, excluded. 2. (subst.) The name of the first Muhammadan month, held sacred on account of the death of Ḥusain, son of Aﻌlī, who was killed by Yazīd, near Kūfa. (Plur. محرمات muḥarramāt, Things forbidden or unlawful). ¶ muḥrim (part. act. IV.) adj. Engaged in the sacred pilgrimage to Mecca, performing the ceremonies enjoined on entering a sacred place. ¶ maḥram (noun of place) s. 1. A spouse, a confidant, an intimate, an intimate friend. 2. Any one who is admitted into the womenʼs apartments or into a sacred place. 3. Bodice, the part of the dress in which the breasts are confined. 4. (used adjectively) Confidential, intimate. محرم راز māḥram-i-rāz, A confidant or confidential person.
subs: s.m. A holiday soldier (so called because fighting was prohibited in that month)
'One born in the month of Muḥarram'; the knight of the rueful countenance.

ety: pl. of muḥarramat, fem. of muḥarram
def: s.m. Things forbidden or unlawful, prohibited things.

ety: for A. محررة muḥarrarat, fem. of muḥarrar
def: part. = muḥarrar, q.v.
more: adj. Written; engrossed. muhar'rirī , n. f. The business of a clerk.

ety: act. part. of حرّق 'to burn,' &c., ii of حرق 'to be burnt,' &c.
def: adj. Inflamed; burning (a fever); — s.m. An incendiary.
more: (part. act. II. of حرق) adj. Inflamed, burning (a fever).
(part. act. IV. from حرق) adj. Burning, inflammatory, ardent.

ety: act. part. of حرّك 'to move, put in motion,' ii of حرك 'to move'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Moving, putting in motion; stirring, motory, stimulant, incentive; — marking a consonant with a vowel; — mover, stirrer up, inciter, instigator, exciter (of); adviser, persuader, tempter; encourager, promoter.
more: n. m. حرک 1. A mover. 2. A promoter. 3. A ringleader.
part. act. (II. of حرک) or muḥrik (of the IV.) Moving, putting in motion, stimulant, incentive. 2. (subst.) A mover, persuader, exciter, tempter, encourager.

ety: corrupt
def: s.m. A writer, clerk, scribe; — muḥarrar (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Written, inscribed; engrossed.
more: (part. act. II. of حرّ Heb. ) s. m. A writer, a clerk. ¶ muḥarrar (part. pass.) Written, inscribed.

def: s.f. The office or business of a muḥarrir or clerk.

ety: S. मुख+माषकः, or मांस+कं
def: s.m. A pimple on the face (esp. of a youth)
subs: Pimples to break out on the face.

def: n. m. pl. muhāse . Cont. of H. muṅh face phunsī pimple. Pimples on the face, of a youth. muhāse nikalnā , v. n. To come out (pimples).
more: s. m. A pimple (on the face in youth).

ety: for A. محاسبة muḥāsabat, inf. n. of حاسب; see muḥāsib
def: s.m. Computation, calculation, account; adjustment or settlement of accounts
more: n. m. حسب computed. Adjustment or settlement of accounts. muhāsaba k. , v. a. To settle accounts.

def: part. adj. & s.m. Trying, testing, proving; — having experience (in affairs); — a trier, prover; experimenter; assayer
more: adj. جرب assayed. Tried; proved; experienced. mujarrab nuskhā , n. A tried prescription; a specific.
part. act. (II. of جرب Chald. ) Trying, proving. ¶ mujarrab (part. pass.) adj. Tried, proved; expert, skilful, experienced.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Tried, proved, tested, examined (as medicine), assayed (as coin); experienced (in), expert, skilled, versed
s.m. A tried prescription; a specific.

ety: pass. part. of جسّم 'to make large, or bulky'; 'to render corporeal,' ii of جسم 'to be or become big or bulky'
def: part. adj. Rendered corporeal, clothed with a body; embodied; incarnate; incorporated; corporeal; corpulent; massy, solid, substantial; — s.m. Substance; (in Geom.) a solid (pl. mujassamāt).
more: adj. جسم became corporeal. 1. Embodied; incorporated. 2. Incarnate. 3. Corporeal. 4. Solid; substantial. mujassam ; Pl. mujassamāt , n. m. 1. A substance. 2. (H. ḍaul-nāp ) ( Geom .) A solid.
(part. pass. II. of جسم Chald. ) adj. Having body, imbodied, corpulent, incorporated, incarnate.

ety: for A. مجاورة, inf. n. of جاور; see mujāwir
def: s.f. Being near, becoming a neighbour; keeping up a good correspondence with neighbours; — neighbourhood; — intimacy; — remaining in a mosque or temple devoutly employed, constant attendance in a mosque, &c.
more: (inf. III. of جور) s. f. 1. Neighbourhood. 2. Constant attendance at temple, &c.

ety: act. part. of جاور 'to become the neighbour of,' &c., iii of جور; but formed from جار jār, 'a neighbour'
def: adj. & s.m. Neighbouring, adjacent; — fixed or confined to a mosque or place of worship (and occupied in prayer and religious meditation), devoutly employed; — a neighbour; — an attendant at a mosque or shrine; one who is constant in prayer and meditation in a mosque; the sweeper of a mosque.
more: n. m. جور being near. An attendant at a Mah. shrine or temple.
(part. act. III. of جور Heb. ) s. m. 1. A neighbour. 2. A sweeper of a mosque, an attendant at a mosque (or other place of worship), one who remains devoutly employed at a mosque or attached to it.

ety: fr. mujāwir, q.v.
def: s.f. Neighbourhood, &c. ( = mujāwarat, q.v.); — the office of attendant at a mosque or shrine.
more: n. f. The office of a mujāvir , q. v.

ety: act. part. of جاوز 'to exceed or transgress' (the proper bound
def: part. adj. Transgressing, exceeding bounds; — immoderate, excessive; (in Gram.) transitive; — s.m. One who transgresses, &c.
more: part. act. (III. of جوز Heb. ) Transgressing, exceeding, bounding.

ety: pass. part. of جوّف 'to make hollow, to hollow out,' ii of جوف 'to be hollow,' &c.
def: part. adj. Hollowed, hollowed out; concave; vaulted; hollow; empty (thing, or place, or fellow), vacant, void; having no heart, cowardly; — wide, spacious, ample.
more: adj. جوق was hollow. Hollow; concave; hollow at the core.

ety: for A. مجوزة mujawwazat, fem. of mujawwaz
def: adj. = mujawwaz, q.v.
adj. 1. Proposed. 2. Decided; determined. 3. Prescribed; laid down. mujavvizān-i-qānūn , A legislature body; legislature; court of legislature; parliament.

ety: act. part. of جوّز 'to cause to pass or be current,' &c., ii of جوز 'to pass,' &c.
def: part. adj. Holding, or proving, to be lawful; allowing to pass, permitting, sanctioning, approving, recommending
more: part. act. (II. of جوز Heb. ) 1. Holding or proving to be lawful. 2. Permitting, allowing to pass, recommending. ¶ mujauwaz, part. pass. Lawful, permitted.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Approved, sanctioned; permitted, tolerated; proposed; decided, determined; prescribed; lawful
s.m. A legislative body; legislature, court of legislature; parliament.

ety: pass. part. of اوْجز 'to abbreviate; to epitomize,' &c., iv of وجز 'to be short, or compendious,' &c.'
def: part. adj. Abbreviated; epitomized; compendious; brief; — s.m. An epitome, a compendium, a summary.
more: (part. pass. IV. of وجز) s. m. A summary, compendium, epitome.

ety: pass. part. of جزّأ, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. Divided into parts or portions.
more: (part. pass. II. of جزأ Heb. ) adj. Divided into parts.

def: pron. (dakh.) Me, to me: see مجهي mujh-e.
pron., a dialec. form of mujhe, q.v.

ety: Prk. gen. मज्झ, or Ap. मज्झु; prob. fr. S. माटृश; or fr. S. मदीय 'mine,' by substitution of Prk. base मह for S. मद्
def: a formative or inflect. base of the pron. maiṅ 'I,' and = ʻme'
subs: 'it has come on me,' 'I am responsible, I am surety' (or I will pay).

def: pron. S. माम् Mujh se us ne aisā kām kīyā . He committed an indecent assault on me. mujh se ; Illit. mijh se ; Rus. matte ; Pr. mutto, maītto, mamādo , To me; from me. mujh meṅ ; Pr. mamammī , In me. mujh meṅ āyā , 1. I am responsible. 2. I am surety for him. 3. I will pay for him. mujhe, mujh ko ; Panj. mai nū ; Rus. man ne, mū ne ; Poet. maīko ; Bhoj. hamrāke . pron. Pr. ahammī . Me; to me. Tū mujh-ko tau maiṅ tujh-ko . Prov. As you treat me so I'll treat you.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa मज्झु (majjhu), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀚𑁆𑀛𑀁 (majjhaṃ), from Sanskrit मह्यम् (mahyam). Cognate with Marathi माझे (māj̈he).

def: pron. obl. (dialec. or rustic) = مجهہ mujh, q.v. a formative or inflect. base of the pron. maiṅ 'I,' and = ʻme'
subs: 'it has come on me,' 'I am responsible, I am surety' (or I will pay).

def: pron. Me, to me. (See مجهـ).

ety: dialec.
def: pron. Me; to me.

ety: act. part. of اجاب 'to answer; to accept,' &c., ii of جوب 'to make a hole in or through,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Granting an answer, responding (to), listening (to), granting, consenting (to), accepting, taking well; — one who listens (to), or grants, &c.
more: (part. act. IV. of جوب) Listening to, granting, consenting, taking well.

ety: act. part. of اوْجب 'to render necessary, &c., iv of وجب 'to be necessary,' &c.
def: s.m. Who or what renders necessary or obligatory; cause, motive, reason, account.
more: n. m. Pl. موجبات Cause; reason.
(part. act. IV. of وجب) s. m. Cause, reason, motive, account.

def: s.m. corr. of موجبه mūjiba, q.v.

ety: for A. موجبة mūjibat, fr. mūjib
def: s.m. A thing of great importance (whether good or bad); great virtue or vice (as deserving of reward or punishment in the world to come); — the rewards or punishments of eternity; — (in Logic) an affirmative proposition (opposed to sāliba).
more: n. m. ( Logic .) An affirmative proposition (opp. of sālibā ).
(part. act. fem. IV. of وجب) s. m. 1. A thing of importance (whether good or bad). 2. The rewards or punishments of eternity. 3. (in logick) An affirmative proposition (opposed to سالبه).

def: (Braj.), s.m. pl. of mūjib; and also of mūjiba.
s.m. pl. (of mūjib), Causes, reasons, motives.

ety: act. part. of اوْجد 'to cause to find; to give existence,' &c., iv of وجد 'to discover,' &c.
def: s.m. Causer, cause, author, inventor
more: n. m. وجد discovering. Cause; author ( bānī ). mūjid h. , v. n. To be the cause of. mūjidān-i-qānūn , A legislative assembly; a Parliament.
(part. act. IV. of وجد) s. m. 1. An author, inventor, causer. 2. Corroborating, reinstating (after sickness or poverty).
subs: (-kā), To be the cause, or author (of)
(mūjidān, Pers. pl. of muʼjid), s.m. A legislative body or assembly, a Parliament.

ety: prop. muʼjid, act. part. of آجد 'to corroborate, strengthen,' &c. iv of أجد 'to be robust,' &c.
def: part. adj. Corroborating, strengthening, invigorating; restoring, or reinstating (after sickness, or poverty).
part. act. (IV. of أجر Heb ) Hiring, letting to farm, renting.

ety: prop. muʼjir, act. part. of آجر, iv of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Letting on hire, hiring; letting to farm; renting.

ety: pass. part. of اجمل, 'to collect; to sum up,' &c., iv of جمل 'to collect'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Brought together, collected into a small compass; contracted; abridged; compendious, brief; — confused, requiring explanation; — an abstract, a summary, compendium, a compendious statement or account (of); a confused statement or account (including a number of things requiring explanation)
more: adj. جمل summed up. Abstracted; summary; brief ( ijmālī ). mujmal hisāb . An abstract account.
(part. pass. IV. of جمل) s. m. An abstract, summary, compendium, abridgment. part. Brought together, contracted, abridged.

ety: acc. of mujmal
def: adv. In substance; in the abstract; in brief, summarily, compendiously; cursorily.
more: adv. In the abstract; compendiously; summarily; in short; cursorily.

ety: for A. مجري mujrā, pass. part. of اجري 'to cause to run, or to flow,' iv of جري 'to run; to flow'
def: part. Carried to account or to credit, credited (to); — appointed as an allowance or maintenance; — s.m. That which is carried to the account or credit (of), credit; — that which is appointed as an allowance, maintenance; allowance, premium, deduction; a set-off (against); — obeisance, respects; visit; audience; presentation; presentation of arms (to); — a dirge; — a trial of professional singing
more: n. m. 1. Deduction; allowance; a set-off. 2. A Mah. dirge. 3. Audience; presentation. 4. Obeisance; respects; presenting arms; kissing the hand. 'Lapaṭ jhapaṭ garyāle meṅ nikrī, jāno chhūṭo bāj sikārī ko, Mujro le chūṅdar-vālī ko, mujro le! Rus. song.' She darted on him in the lane as a hawk upon his prey, 'my salutations, pray accept, accept!' 5. A trial of professional singing. Mujrā huā thā yā nāch? mujrā pānā , v. n. To receive credit (for a sum). mujrā-talbī . A counter-claim. mujrā k. , v. a. To wait on one; pay one's respects to; make obeisance; present arms. mujrā k., yā denā , v. a. 1. To allow abatement or deductions. 2. To give credit (for a sum). mujrā lenā , v. n. To deduct; subtract. mujrā h. , v. n. To be deducted, retrenched. mujrā yā chhūṭ chāhnā . To claim a credit or set-off.

(part. pass. IV. of جري lit. Caused to flow, rendered current) s. m. 1. (In Pers. and Hind.) Allowance, premium, deduction. 2. (in Hind.) Obeisance, respects, visit, audience. مجرا کرنا mujrā karnā, To make obeisance, pay respects, wait on, visit. مجريگاه mujre-gāh, Place of visiting, audience, salutation, &c.
subs: (-kā), To receive credit (for a sum); — to receive an allowance, &c.
(-kā), To claim credit, or abatement, or a set-off (for)
(-ko), To give credit (for a sum); to grant an allowance; to allow an abatement or a deduction (to)
v.t. To give (one) credit (for); — to make allowance, or deduction, to allow for, &c. ( = mujrā denā); — to make obeisance (to), pay respects (to), to wait on; to present arms (to), to salute
v.n. To be carried to account or credit; to be appointed as an allowance, &c.; — to be deducted, to be retrenched
s.i. Place of visiting; audience; salutation.

def: part. adj. & s.m. = mujrā, q.v. part. Carried to account or to credit, credited (to); — appointed as an allowance or maintenance; — s.m. That which is carried to the account or credit (of), credit; — that which is appointed as an allowance, maintenance; allowance, premium, deduction; a set-off (against); — obeisance, respects; visit; audience; presentation; presentation of arms (to); — a dirge; — a trial of professional singing
subs: (-kā), To receive credit (for a sum); — to receive an allowance, &c.
(-kā), To claim credit, or abatement, or a set-off (for)
(-ko), To give credit (for a sum); to grant an allowance; to allow an abatement or a deduction (to)
v.t. To give (one) credit (for); — to make allowance, or deduction, to allow for, &c. ( = mujrā denā); — to make obeisance (to), pay respects (to), to wait on; to present arms (to), to salute
v.n. To be carried to account or credit; to be appointed as an allowance, &c.; — to be deducted, to be retrenched
s.i. Place of visiting; audience; salutation.

def: a corr. form of مجرا mujrā, q.v.

ety: mujrā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका; or a Pers. form.; ˚ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Allowance, deduction; credit given on account; remission (of revenue, &c.), reduction (of assessment).

ety: mujrā + ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. One who pays his respects, one who waits upon; one who is presented (to); a servant; minister; visitor, &c.; — one who recites a mars̤iya or dirge.
s. m. One who pays his respects; a servant, minister, visitant, &c.
s.f. contrac. of mujrāʼī, q.v.
n. m. 1. Remission of revenue; reduction in the assessment. 2. A credit given on account. 3. One who waits upon or is presented. 4. One who recites the marsiyā or Mah. dirge.

ety: act. part. of اجرم, iv of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Criminal; culpable; — a criminal, delinquent, an offender, a felon
more: n. m. جرم pl. mujrimān . A criminal; delinquent; offender; felon. mujrim ṭhairānā, yā qarār denā . To convict; sentence. mujrim h. , v. n. To commit an offence.
(part. act. IV. of جرم Heb. ) adj. Criminal; (subst.) A sinner.
subs: or mujrim qarār denā (-ko), To prove (one) an offender, &c.; to convict; to sentence
To become a criminal or an offender, to commit an offence.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُجْرِم (mujrim).

ety: mujrim, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. Criminal, culpable (e.g. ʻilm-ě-mujrimāna, 'criminal knowledge').

ety: pass. part. of اجتبي, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. Chosen; elected; elect.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of جبو or جبي Heb. ) adj. Elected, chosen, elect.

ety: act. part. of اجتہد, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Striving; laborious, painstaking; — contending (against), waging war (esp. against infidels); — s.m. A superior or superintendent in religious matters, spiritual director, a prelate; — the highest degree to which the learned in (Moḥammadan) law attain; the highest authority in jurisprudence; a jurist (pl. mujtahidīn).
n. m. جـﮩد striving. 1. A superintendent in religious matters; aspiritual director; a prelate. 2. A jurist; the highest authority in Mah. jurisprudence.
more: part. act. (VIII. of جـﮩد) Striving, contending, carrying on war especially against infidels; (subst.) A superiour or superintendent in religious matters, prelate.

ety: mujtahid+Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. Prelatical; patriarchal; in the manner of a spiritual director.

ety: act. part. of اجتمع 'to become collected, or brought together,' &c., viii of جمع 'to collect,' &c.
def: part. adj. In a state of collection, or congregation, &c.; met or come together, assembled, convened, congregated; — agreeing (or agreed) in opinion; — (a man) who has attained to his full state of manly vigour; — s.m. An assemblage, a body (of persons), a company.
more: (part. act. VIII. of جمع Chald. ) 1. adj. Assembled, convened, together in one place. 2. Agreeing in the same opinion. 3. (A man) arriving at full strength. 4. s. m. An assemblage.

ety: act. part. of اجترم, viii of (and syn. with
def: adj. Guilty, criminal.
more: (part. act. VIII. of جرم) adj. Guilty.

def: s.m. (dialec.) prob. corr. of mukh, q.v. (cf. muk, and mūkā, 'opening,' &c.).

ety: Prk. मुक्को; S. मूकः
def: adj. Speechless, dumb; silent; mute; — poor, wretched; — a dumb person; a mute; — a dumb creature, or animal; a fish; — a poor man, a pauper; — name of a Dānav or Daitya.
s.m. = مکا mukkā, q.v. s.m. The fist; — a fist, a blow with the fist; thump, buffet
more: s. m. 1. The fist. 2. A thump, blow. 3. (from मुख ) A spy-hole.
subs: v.n. The fist to be used; to come to blows
(-ko), To receive a blow with the fist; — to suffer a shock
(-ko), To strike a blow with the fist; to give (one) a buffet.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = mūkhā, or mokhā, q.v. prop. n. The town of Mocha (on the west coast of Arabia).

ety: pass. part. of کعّب 'to make of a cubic form,' ii of کعب, but formed fr. kaʻb, 'a cube'
def: adj. Of a square or cubic form; cubical.

ety: pass. part. of قعّر 'to make deep,' &c., ii of قعر 'to be deep'
def: part. adj. Made deep; excavated; deep, profound; — hollow; concave.
s.m. = مقابا muqābā, q.v. s.m. A dressing-box, or dressing-case.

ety: corr. of A. مقوّي muqawuā, q.v.
def: s.m. A dressing-box, or dressing-case.
more: s. m. A dressing-box or case. See مقابه muḳāba.

def: n. m. A dressing case. Muqābā koī khol missī lagāë, Laboṅ par dhaṛī koī apne jamāë . Mīr Hasan.

ety: for A. مقابلة muqābalat, inf. n. of قابل 'to stand over against, to face,' &c., iii of قبل 'to look to the tip of the nose,' &c.; see qabl
def: s.m. Facing, standing over against; meeting face to face, confronting; opposing, resisting, contending; — putting side by side, comparing, collating; — presence; opposition, opposite quarter; resistance; encounter, contest; competition; comparison; contrast; comptrol; collation; examination
more: n. m. قبل went before. 1. Opposition. 2. Comparison. 3. Encounter. muqāblah aur imtehān , Check; audit. muqāble par mustaid h . G. G. To resist. muqāblah chāhnā yā k., muqāble ānā , v. n. To challenge; call to combat; defy. muqāblah k. , v. a. 1. To confront; oppose; contend with. 2. To compete or cope with. 3. To compare; collate. 4. To balance; examine; check, compare (accounts, papers, etc.).

(inf. III. of قبل Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Comparison, collation, competition, opposition, confronting, contest, contending, presence. مقابله کرنا muḳābala karnā, To contend, confront, oppose, emulate, compare, collate. مقابله هونا muḳābala honā, To be in front, to confront, meet, encounter.
subs: (-ke), To come in front (of), to face, confront; to oppose; to challenge to combat, to defy
To be ready or prepared to oppose, or resist, or contend (with)
(-se), To demand or desire to be put face to face (with); to challenge, to defy to combat; to desire comparison, &c.

(-kā, or -se), To confront; to oppose, resist, to contend (with, or against); to compete, or cope (with); — to challenge; — to compare, collate, contrast; to examine, check, or balance (accounts, &c.).

ety: for A. مکابرة mukābarat, inf. n. of کابر 'to lift up; to contend,' iii of کبر 'to be great,' &c.
def: s.m. Pride, haughtiness; — contention.

ety: act. part. of کبّر 'to make, or call, or esteem great; to magnify,' ii of کبر 'to be great'
def: adj. & s.m. Magnifying (God), repeating the formula allāhǒ akbar, 'God is very great'; — one who magnifies (God, by saying allāhǒ akbar).
more: (part. act. II. of کبر) adj. Magnifying; one who magnifies (God, saying الله اکبر).

ety: act. part. of قاَبَل; see muqābala
def: adj. (f. -kī), Opposite, &c.
more: adj. قبل 1. Confronting; opposing; against (اَگے 2, 3). 2. Matching; corresponding; like.
(part. act. III. of قبل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Opposite, confronting, opposing, matching, resembling, equal, comparing: (postpos. mas.) in opposition, against, for.

ety: pass. part. of قدّم 'to give precedence to; to prefer,' &c., ii of قدم 'to precede,' &c.
def: part. adj. Given precedence; placed before; preferred; — preceding, antecedent, prior; — superior; chief; more important; — s.m. Advanced guard (of an army); — major premiss (of a syllogism); — superior officer of the revenue (in a village), a village head; a chief, leader; — a title of respect among villagers
more: adj. قدم preceding. See pahlā . 1. Antecedent; before. 2. Prior; preceding. 3. Superior; chief. muqqad'dam ; Rus. mukdam , n. m. 1. A village head. 2. A title of respect among villagers. muqaddam jānnā , v. n. To consider preferable. muqaddam h. , v. n. To be first or most binding. tārīkh muqaddam kar-ke likhnā , G. G. H. pahlī mitī ḍālnā yā chaṛhānā , v. n. To antedate.

(part. pass. II. of قدم Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Antecedent, prior, preceding; s. m. The advanced guard, a chief, a leader. مقدم جاننا muḳaddam jānnā, To prefer, to consider preferable (over). مقدم هونا muḳaddam honā, To precede. ¶ maḳdam (noun of place or time) s. m. Arrival.
subs: v.t. To consider preferable (to or over, -par); to deem superior (to), or more important, &c.
v.t. To place before, put first; to give (one, or a thing) preference (over, -par), to prefer
(-par), To stand (before); to precede; to take precedence (of); to be first, or most binding.

ety: for A. مقدمة muqaddamat, fem. of muqaddam, q.v.
def: s.m. The first part; preamble (to a speech); preface (to a book); prelude; introduction; premisses (of an argument); preliminary; — affair, matter, case, business, subject, topic, thesis; — law-suit, suit, cause, case, proceedings; prosecution; — advanced guard (of an army)
more: n. m. قدم going before. 2. Preliminary; preamble; prelude; introduction. 2. Subject ( bāb , 2). Rāt kā muqadmah hai, nihatte na chalo! 3. A law suit; case; proceedings prosecution (دعوي 3). muqadmah banānā, yā uṭhānā , v. a. To get up a case. muqadmā jītnā , v. n. To win a case. muqadmah khaṛā k. , To institute a suit; pursue by law; prosecute; litigate. muqadmah laṛānā , v. a. To go to law; litigate; conduct a suit. maqadmah hārnā , v. n. To lose one's cause.

(part. pass. II. of قدم) s. m. 1. The first part. 2. Preamble, preface, preliminary. 3. Cause (in law). 4. Business. 5. Subject, affair, matter, thesis. مقدمة الجيش muḳaddamatu-l-jaish, The advanced guard of an army. مقدمه فہمي muḳaddama-fahm-ī, s. f. The understanding of the subject or business.
subs: or muqaddama banānā, To get up a case
s.f. The understanding of a case, or business, or subject, &c.
To institute a suit; to prosecute; to go to law (with); to litigate
(-se), To fight a case or cause (with); to go to law (with); to litigate; to prosecute
To lose a cause, or suit, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مقدمه (muqaddama), from a variant of Arabic مُقَدِّمة (muqaddima).

def: s.m. pl. (of next, q.v.), Preambles, prefaces, premisses, preliminaries; pre-requisites; — law-suits, cases; — topics.
more: s. plur. of مقدمه q. v.

def: adj. Of or relating to a Muqaddam, or chief of a village, &c.; — s.f. The office of a Muqaddam; the dues, or rights, of a Muqaddam.

ety: pass. part. of قدّر 'to decree, ordain,' &c., ii of قدر 'to measure; to estimate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Decreed (by God), appointed, ordained, destined, predestined, predetermined; — understood, implied; — s.m. Fate, destiny
more: (part. pass. II. of قدر) adj. 1. Predestined. 2. Understood. 3. (subst.) Fate, destiny. ¶ muḳaddir (part. act.) Predestining; (subst.) The deity (as predestinator).
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Decreeing, ordaining, predestining, &c.; — s.m. The Predestinator, God; — the Designer, or Projector, &c.

ety: pass. part. of کدّر 'to render turbid,' &c., ii of کدر 'to be or become turbid,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rendered muddy or turbid; muddy, turbid; — disturbed, troubled, afflicted; — vexed, displeased; — sullen, gloomy (cf. malīn)
more: adj. 1. Turbid ( gadlā ). 2. Disturbed; troubled; sullen; gloomy. 3. Vexed; displeased.
(part. pass. II. of کدر) adj. Muddy; sullen, gloomy, troubled, afflicted, disturbed, vexed, anxious.
subs: (-kī t̤araf-se), To be disturbed, or troubled, &c. (on account of, or with regard to).

ety: pass. part. of قدّس 'to hallow, sanctify,' &c., ii of قدس 'to be holy'
def: part. adj. Hallowed, sanctified, consecrated; holy; purified; blessed
more: adj. قدس Hallowed; consecrated, kitāb-i-muqaddas , n. f. Holy Scripture.
(part. pass. II. of قدس Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Holy, consecrated. بيت المقدس baitu-l-muḳaddas, Jerusalem. ¶ maḳdis (noun of place) A holy place.
subs: 'The hallowed dwelling,' or 'the dwelling of the hallowed,' &c., i.e. Jerusalem
The holy or blessed book, Holy Scripture.

def: (for A. مقدسة muqaddasat, fem. of muqaddas), part. fem. = muqaddas, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مُقَدَّسَة (muqaddasa), feminine of مُقَدَّس (muqaddas).

ety: for A. مکافاة, inf. n. of کافّي 'to recompense, compensate, requite,' &c., ii of کفّي 'to be sufficient,' &c.
def: s.f. Compensation, reparation; recompense, requital; retribution; retaliation
more: n. f. کفي sufficient. Reparation ( badlā , 2. 3.). mukāfāt meṅ , By way of reparation; as. mukāfāt meṅ denā , v. a. To indemnify.
(inf. III. of کفي) s. f. Recompense, retribution, retaliation, requital.
subs: adv. By way of compensation, or reparation, &c.

ety: for A. مقفَي muqaffā, pass. part. of قفّي 'to make (one thing
def: part. adj. & s.m. Rhythmically composed (oration); rhyming or measured (prose); — a rhythmically composed oration (syn. musajjaʻ).
more: adj. قفو Rhythmical (prose). muqaffa o musajjā , Metrical and rhymed.
(for مقفيٰ part. pass. II. of قفو) adj. 1. Measured (prose). 2. (subst.) An oration rhythmically composed.

ety: pass. part. of قفّل 'to lock,' &c., ii of قفل; but prob. formed fr. qufl, 'a lock'
def: part. adj. Locked; bolted (as a door, &c.)
more: Ped. adj. Locked. muqaffal k. , Ped. v. a. To lock up ( tālā denā ).
(part. pass. II. of قفل Chald. and Aram. ) adj. Locked.
subs: v.t. To lock, lock up; to bolt (a door).

ety: act. part. of قاعد 'to sit with; to guard,' &c., iii of (and also syn. with
def: s.m. Guardian, preserver, keeper; — whatever remains or comes behind (another, as a doe, or a bird, &c.).
more: (part. act. III. of قعد) s. m. 1. Whatever comes behind another. 2. A guardian, keeper.

ety: pass. part. of قيّد 'to put shackles (qaid
def: part. adj. Shackled, fettered, in chains; bound; imprisoned, confined; restrained; restricted; strict; — fixed; determined; — attentive (to), solicitous, earnest, diligent; dedicated (to); addicted (to); — pointed with syllabical signs (as a writing or letters); — registered, recorded, written, noted, signed, marked.
more: (part. pass. II. of قيد) adj. Diligent, attentive, earnest, fixed, bound, fettered, in chains, imprisoned, strict, dedicated to, addicted, noted, signed, registered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُقَیَّد (muqayyad), borrowed from Arabic مُقَيِّد (muqayyid), from قَيَّدَ (qayyada).

ety: act. part. of قيّأ 'to cause to vomit,' ii of قآءَ (for قيأ
def: adj. & s.m. = muqī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Causing vomiting, vomitive (medicine); — an emetic.
more: (part. act. II. of قيأ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Emetick (a medicine).

def: W. The altar on which the first fruits of the sugar press are offered up to Bābū Mukāl.

ety: pass. part. of کلّف 'to put to trouble or inconvenience,' &c., ii of کلف 'to attend carefully (to anything
def: adj. Busied, occupied with care; — well-made, exquisitely wrought; done with great pains and care; elaborated; elaborate; decorated, ornamented; elegant; excellent; choice
more: adj. کلف 1. Decorated; ornamented. 2. Elaborate; exquisitely wrought. al mukallaf , Yours obediently.
(part. pass. II. of کلف) adj. Exquisitely wrought, done with great pains and care, elaborate. ¶ mukallif (part. act.) adj. Putting to inconvenience or trouble.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Putting (or one who puts) to inconvenience or trouble (taklīf)

ety: pass. part. of کلّل 'to crown,' &c., ii of کلّ; but formed fr. iklīl, 'a crown'
def: part. adj. Crowned; adorned with jewels; — edged, bordered.
more: (part. pass. II. of کلّ) adj. Crowned, adorned with jewels.
